'''Tinirau Arona''' (born 8 May 1989) is a former<ref name=Yahoo2022a>{{cite web |url=https://uk.news.yahoo.com/mixed-emotions-cook-islands-world-173129072.html |title=Mixed emotions for Cook Islands after World Cup exit |publisher=Yahoo News |date=1 November 2022 |access-date=16 July 2023}}</ref> [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] international<ref name="Tinirau Arona">{{cite web|url=http://www.newtownjets.com.au/2010-gallery/Tinirau-Arona-profile.htm|title=Tinirau Arona|publisher=Newtown Jets|year=2010|access-date=2010-09-03}}</ref> [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Arona was born in [[Dunedin]], New Zealand. He is of [[Cook Islander|Cook Islands]] descent and moved to [[Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]] as an 11-year-old. Arona is cousins with [[Penrith Panthers]] and [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] representative player [[Tupou Sopoaga]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/cronulla-sharks-cousins-tinirau-arona-and-tupou-sopoaga-ready-to-take-on-nrl/story-fni3gol8-1226849555071|title=Cronulla Sharks Cousins' Tinirau Arona and Tupou Sopoaga Ready to Take on NRL|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[St Clair Comets]], before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/after-heading-east-for-new-turf-young-rooster-has-his-hands-full/story-fnca0von-1226444275150?nk=cb04980ce02ce836252030daa4dca687|title=After Heading East for New Turf Young Rooster has His Hands Full|publisher=Theaustralian.com.au|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref>

==Playing career==
On 4 October 2006, Arona made his international début for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] as a 17-year-old, playing off the interchange bench and scored the Cook Islands only try in the 46–6 loss to the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/world-cup-qualifiers-2006/game-4/samoa-vs-cook-islands.html|title=Samoa vs. Cook Islands|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> In October 2009, Arona played 2 matches for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in the Pacific Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/samoa-and-cook-islands-announce-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/57091/default.aspx|title=Samoa and Cook Islands announce squads - NRL.com|publisher=M.nrl.com|date=2009-10-12|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Arona played in the [[Penrith Panthers]] NYC team in 2008–2009<ref>[http://rleague.com/players/Tinirau-Arona_2005/Matches/]  {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140903113509/http://rleague.com/players/Tinirau-Arona_2005/Matches/|date=3 September 2014}}</ref> before moving on to play for the [[Sydney Roosters]] [[NSW Cup]] team the [[Newtown Jets]] in 2010 where Arona was awarded the club's best and fairest and best forward.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2010-news/2010-news-Newtown-Awards-Night-300910.php|title=Established 1908 – Celebrating 102 years of rugby league tradition|publisher=Newtown Jets|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Earning himself a contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]].

==Sydney Roosters==
===2011===
In Round 4 of the [[2011 NRL season]], Arona made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for the [[Sydney Roosters]], playing off the interchange bench in the Roosters 24–6 victory over the [[Wests Tigers]] at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-take-on-the-injury-hit-wests-tigers-in-a-game-of-old-rivals-at-the-sfs/story-fn2mcuj6-1226032835413?nk=cb04980ce02ce836252030daa4dca687|title=Roosters Take On the Injury Hit Tigers|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Arona finished his début year in the [[NRL]] with him playing in 13 matches for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[2011 NRL season]]. On 3 November 2011 The annual [[RLIF Awards]] dinner was held at the [[Tower of London]] and Arona was named [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]]' player of the year.<ref>{{cite news|title=Slater scoops player award|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/15313/7289077/Slater-scoops-player-award|access-date=5 November 2011|newspaper= [[Sky Sports]]|date=3 November 2011}}</ref>
[[File:Tinirau Arona 2012.jpg|thumb|right|Arona playing for the Roosters in 2012]]

===2012===
In Round 9 against the [[Newcastle Knights]] at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]], Arona scored his first NRL career try in the Roosters 24–6 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/pearce-takes-roosters-to-top-eight-spot-in-24-6-win-over-knights/story-fnca0von-1226348048845Arona|title=Pearce Takes Roosters to Top Eight Spot|publisher=Theaustralian.com.au|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Arona finished the [[2012 NRL season]] with him playing in 20 matches and scoring 3 tries (12p) for the [[Sydney Roosters]].

===2013===
In July 2013, Arona represented the NSW Residents side.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mightybears.com/viewtopic.php?p=37648&sid=0faa2cba6535bcc78adb2a043cbb89ff|title=View topic – VB NSW Cup Rep Squad named|publisher=The Mighty Bears|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Arona finished the [[2013 NRL season]] with him playing in 3 matches for the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Arona was part of the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team]] that participated in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] playing at [[Rugby league positions#lock|lock]] in all 3 matches for the Cook Islands.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/player/475|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131110145518/http://www.rlwc2013.com/player/475|url-status=usurped|archive-date=10 November 2013|title=Rugby League World Cup 2013|publisher=Rlwc2013.com|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> On 6 December 2013, Arona signed with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] after getting released from his contract with the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Arona|first=Tinirau|title=TINIRAU ARONA MOVES TO THE CRONULLA-SUTHERLAND SHARKS|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/rugby-league/players/tinirau-arona/|work=Zero Tackle|publisher=Personal Tweet|access-date=5 December 2013}}</ref>

==Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks==

===2014===
In round 1 of the [[2014 NRL season]], Arona made his [[National Rugby League]] Premiership début for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], scoring a try in Cronulla's 18–12 loss to the [[Gold Coast Titans]] at [[Remondis Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/paul-gallen-injures-ankle-in-gold-coast-titans-1812-upset-of-cronulla-sharks-on-monday/story-fni3fbgz-1226850648399|title=Paul Gallen Injures Ankle in Titans 18–12 Upset of Sharks|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref> Arona finished his first year with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 21 matches and scoring 3 tries (12p).  The club endured one of their toughest ever seasons in 2014 finishing with the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News}}</ref>

==Wakefield Trinity==
On 5 November 2015, Arona signed a one-year contract with [[Super League]] side [[Wakefield Trinity]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/wildcats-seal-signing-of-tinirau-arona-19596/#1UIreXStOJIItIFd.97|title=Wildcats seal signing of Tinirau Arona | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=2015-11-05}}</ref>
In 2018, he signed a contract extension to remain at Wakefield until the end of the [[2019 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/11452167/tinirau-arona-signs-new-contract-with-wakefield-trinity/|title=Tinirau Arona signs new contract with Wakefield Trinity|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
In 2019, he signed a three-year contract extension to remain at Wakefield until the end of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wakefieldtrinity.com/blog/2019/07/01/arona-to-remain-at-trinity-until-the-end-of-2022/ |title=Arona to remain at Trinity until the end of 2022|website=wakefieldtrinity.com}}</ref>
On 24 August 2022, Arona announced he would depart Wakefield Trinity at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tinirau-arona-leaves-wakefield-trinity/|title=Tinirau Arona: Cook Islands prop to leave Wakefield|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In Arona's final professional game, he scored a try and kicked the last conversion of the match in the Cook Islands 92–10 loss against Tonga at the [[Riverside Stadium]]. It was Cook Islands' worst ever international result, as of 2023. The Cook Islands finished their [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign in third place in Group D, and were eliminated.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63448198|title=Tonga 92–10 Cook Islands: Tonga close Rugby League World Cup group in style|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

He retired from league at the end of the world cup in November 2022.<ref name=Yahoo2022a/>
